WHS manuals, policies, sample checklists and forms

instructions: Use the provided templates to complete the required Schedules.
Provide the requested information using the DOCX file and submit as a PDF.
If a schedule does not have enough space, include the information in a separate attachment and reference it in the schedule.
Unless otherwise stated, all documents (schedules and supporting documents) must be submitted in PDF format.
Prices in Schedule 10 must be clear and unambiguous; replace zero/blank with 'included' or 'excluded' and provide an explanation if necessary. Council may reject tenders with ambiguous pricing.

schedule_11_corporate_social_responsibil.csr_policy_or_plan_summary: BMG Air Conditioning is committed to doing its part towards the upholding of a strong social cohesion, and we strive to take steps towards the protection & improvement of the environment, communities, and human rights. One of BMG’s recently completed projects was undertaken at the Albion Centre in Surry Hills, which provides clinical services to those affected by HIV and high-impact infectious diseases, including psychology & counselling, HIV treatment & management, nutritional services, and STI screening & treatment. Our participation in this project provided environmental comfort to many of our community’s most vulnerable members during uncomfortable & stressful times.
schedule_11_corporate_social_responsibil.recent_initiative_example: One of BMG’s recently completed projects was undertaken at the Albion Centre in Surry Hills, which provides clinical services to those affected by HIV and high-impact infectious diseases, including psychology & counselling, HIV treatment & management, nutritional services, and STI screening & treatment. Our participation in this project provided environmental comfort to many of our community’s most vulnerable members during uncomfortable & stressful times.

sections.heading: Company Details
fields: Entity Type (e.g., Pty Ltd, Partnership, Sole Trader)

sections.heading: Declaration
sections.content: I/We declare that we have read and understood the Conditions of Tendering and agree to be bound by them, that we are solvent and not under any form of external administration, that we have disclosed any conflicts of interest, that we hold or will obtain the required insurances and licences, and that this tender constitutes a firm offer for the period specified.

sections.heading: Financial Statements
sections.content: Provide annual financial statements (balance sheet and profit/loss) for the last three years. If statements cannot be provided, explain why and supply alternative evidence of financial capacity.

sections.heading: Confidentiality
sections.content: Acknowledge that all information provided by Council is confidential and must not be disclosed except as permitted.

sections.heading: Conflict of Interest
sections.content: Disclose any actual, potential or perceived conflicts of interest that may influence your ability to perform the contract. Include details of the conflict and the proposed mitigation strategy.

sections.heading: ICAC Guidance
sections.content: Refer to NSW ICAC guidance on managing conflicts of interest in procurement for further information.

sections.heading: Supporting Documents
sections.content: Attach curriculum vitae and/or licences evidencing qualifications and experience for each proposed key personnel.

rows: Workers’ Compensation (as required under NSW law)

sections.heading: Subcontractor Insurances
sections.content: List insurances held by all subcontractors. Tenderers may be required to increase cover limits if Council deems necessary.

sections.heading: Relevant Projects
sections.content: Provide details of at least three relevant projects completed in the last five years, including client name, project description, contract value, start and finish dates, and your scope of work.

sections.heading: Councils Serviced
sections.content: List any councils or local government organisations you are currently providing services to and the number of years you have been engaged.

sections.heading: Client Referees
sections.content: Provide details for at least two senior client referees (up to four may be provided).

sections.heading: Methodology & Resources
sections.content: Describe your methodology for delivering the works, including sequencing of activities, resource allocations (personnel, plant, equipment, suppliers and subcontractors), and how you will ensure quality outcomes.

sections.heading: Quality & Accreditations
sections.content: Outline your quality management system, including reference to any third‑party accreditations and inspection test plans (ITPs). Attach sample reports where available.

sections.heading: Milestones

rows: Delivery and installation of major equipment
Handover and defects liability period commencement

sections.content: Populate the milestones table with the key deliverables for this project. If Council has mandated a fixed date for commencement or completion (e.g., start of operations by TBA), ensure your program aligns with that date.

sections.heading: Disruption Minimisation
sections.content: Explain how you will minimise disruption to Council operations and stakeholders during the works.

sections.heading: Q&A Confirmations
sections.content: Confirm that you will provide the following information as part of your tender or prior to award: product catalogues/technical sheets, draft layout diagrams/drawings, crew qualifications and experience, warranty terms, equipment origin (country of manufacture), and details of any proposed subcontractors.

sections.heading: Performance / Technical Specification
sections.content: List the key performance requirements and technical specifications for the major equipment or services to be supplied. For example, device capacities, ratings, compliance standards, control interfaces, and any basis‑of‑design references.

sections.heading: Pricing Table
sections.content: Provide a lump sum price in Australian dollars for each scope package. Pricing must include GST, levies, duties, overheads and all other costs necessary to perform the works. Prices must be valid for the tender validity period.

sections.heading: CSR Policy / Initiatives
sections.content: Describe your organisation’s corporate social responsibility policy or a relevant CSR initiative.

sections.heading: Sustainability Practices
sections.content: Explain your organisation’s understanding of sustainability and outline the practices or initiatives you have in place to support sustainable procurement and operations.

sections.heading: Policies & Evidence
sections.content: Provide copies of your organisation’s policies and evidence of practices relating to equal employment opportunity and fair employment. This may include recruitment and training policies, anti‑discrimination and harassment policies, consultation and dispute resolution processes.

sections.heading: Industrial Relations & Commitments
sections.content: State any past breaches of wage or superannuation obligations and confirm rectification. Provide your commitments to employing Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, apprentices and trainees, and your acknowledgement of the Industrial Relations Undertaking.

sections.heading: Acknowledgement of Council WHS Policy
sections.content: Acknowledge that you have read Council’s WHS Policy and will comply with all WHS obligations applicable to the contract.

sections.heading: Required WHS Evidence
sections.content: Provide copies of your WHS manuals, policies and procedures; descriptions of WHS responsibilities and consultation processes; sample checklists and inspection forms; records of WHS incidents and corrective actions; training records; risk assessments/SWMS; registers and SDS; plant and equipment maintenance records; permit‑to‑work samples; and a site‑specific WHS plan for a similar project.

sections.heading: Chain of Responsibility (CoR)
sections.content: If transport of goods is involved, provide evidence of your Chain of Responsibility compliance, including a CoR policy, risk register, KPIs, training records, fleet maintenance and diary records, and subcontractor pre‑engagement audits.

sections.heading: Required WHS Forms
sections.content: Attach the completed WHS Forms (Appendices 1–3) and WHS Policy (Appendix 4).

sections.heading: Supplier Declarations
sections.content: Provide details of whether your organisation is reporting under the Australian Modern Slavery Act. Attach any modern slavery policies and describe your due diligence processes, training initiatives and supply chain risk assessments. List the countries of manufacture for products or components relevant to this tender.

sections.heading: Addenda Acknowledgement
sections.content: List all addenda issued for this RFT and indicate acknowledgment.

sections.heading: Declaration
sections.content: I/We declare that we have received all addenda listed above, that our tender complies with all requirements of the tender documents except as noted, that the information provided in all returnable schedules is true and correct, and that our offer remains open for the tender validity period (e.g., 120 days or as specified).

schedule_3_confidentiality_and_conflict_.confidentiality_basis: You may only disclose information about the Contract if it is: • already in the public domain, other than as a result of your own acts or omissions • required by law, court order, tribunal or another regulatory body, as long as you notify us of the need to disclose this.

schedule_12_sustainability.sustainability_understanding: Sustainability is the concept of operating in a way that maintains performance while minimising negative impacts, whether they be economic, societal, or environmental, in order to ensure that future generations are able to persist unimpeded by our actions. BMG is committed to maximising the sustainability of our practices and as such we are Carbon LCA certified and strive to minimise our environmental impacts by recycling materials wherever possible, replacing diesel & petrol vehicles with electric vehicles (EVs), installing energy-efficient HVAC solutions, and eliminating the use of harmful refrigerants
schedule_12_sustainability.organisational_practices_and_examples: Sustainability is the concept of operating in a way that maintains performance while minimising negative impacts, whether they be economic, societal, or environmental, in order to ensure that future generations are able to persist unimpeded by our actions.
